title: Semiperiodoc cleanup of obsolete FIXMEs
body:
Some refer to issues that are closed. Some, I have no idea if
they're fixed or not, we'll find out in CI.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

```release-note
Increase test coverage by removing outdated skips
```

comments.body:
@containers/podman-maintainers @adrianreber  any idea what's going on here? TL;DR checkpoint tests are [failing in ubuntu SYSTEM tests](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5410976672514048/html/sys-podman-ubuntu-2204-root-host.log.html) but [passing in ubuntu INT tests](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5586898532958208/html/int-podman-ubuntu-2204-root-host.log.html).

Context is that I found a stray skip/FIXME, about checkpoint tests not working in Ubuntu, and removed the skip because I thought checkpointing was working on ubuntu. Is this a filesystem thing? system tests use `/var` and int tests use `/tmp`
